content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

The amendment identifies a real thresholding defect in proposed-bead reconciliation: current counting can treat duplicates from one source as independent support.

## Votes

- `fr-6-5968daff`: Accept
  The cited implementation groups propose-new-bead findings by normalized title and thresholds on the sum of occurrence_count. Review-stage findings are each assigned occurrence_count = 1, so duplicate findings from one payload can satisfy the default N=2 policy. Final-review amendments can similarly inflate support if one source contributes multiple accepted amendments with the same title. The policy is source-based, so the fix should count distinct stable source identities per proposal group.
